draft.introduction: The risen Christ opens the disciples’ minds to understand the Scriptures and commissions them as witnesses. Luke’s ending connects directly to the beginning of Acts, forming a unified narrative.

translation.text: Then he said to them, “These are my words that I spoke to you while I was still with you, that everything written about me in the Law of Moses and the Prophets and the Psalms must be fulfilled.” Then he opened their minds to understand the Scriptures, and said to them, “Thus it is written, that the Christ should suffer and on the third day rise from the dead, and that repentance for the forgiveness of sins should be proclaimed in his name to all nations, beginning from Jerusalem. You are witnesses of these things.”

translator_notes.note: The threefold division—“Law of Moses, Prophets, and Psalms”—is the earliest reference to the tripartite structure of the Hebrew Bible (Torah, Nevi’im, Ketuvim). Luke presents Jesus as the interpretive key to all three divisions.
